title: Brussels Airport Guide
description: Everything you need to know about arriving at Brussels Airport (BRU).

items.name: Brussels Airport (BRU), Zaventem

transportOptions.priceNote: Includes the Brussels Airport rail supplement; also stops at Brussels-North and Brussels-South (Midi).
transportOptions.howTo: Follow 'Train' signs from the arrivals hall down to the station beneath the terminal. Buy tickets at SNCB machines or the SNCB app before boarding.

transportOptions.line: STIB/MIVB Airport Line (Bus 12 / Night bus N12)
transportOptions.price: €7.90 with MOBIB or contactless card, €8.40 paper ticket
transportOptions.priceNote: Cheaper and simpler than the train if your destination is near the EU Quarter.
transportOptions.howTo: Bus stop is outside the arrivals hall. Tap a contactless bank card or MOBIB card directly, or buy a ticket from the driver/machine.

transportOptions.duration: ~25-40 minutes to city centre depending on traffic
transportOptions.priceNote: Only use taxis from the official rank with visible meters; avoid unmarked touts inside the terminal.
transportOptions.howTo: Follow 'Taxi' signs to the official rank outside arrivals.

immigrationTips: Non-EU passport holders use the 'All Passports' lanes; e-gates are for EU/EEA/Swiss passports only.
Have your passport, visa, return ticket and proof of accommodation ready in case of questions.
Automated passport control kiosks are being rolled out for some visa categories — check current signage on arrival.
customsRules: Non-EU travellers can bring in duty-free goods up to certain value/quantity limits (check current allowances on the Belgian customs website before travel).
Declare cash amounts of €10,000 or more when entering or leaving the EU.
Certain food items (meat, dairy) from outside the EU are restricted or prohibited.
simCardKiosks: Proximus, Orange and Base/Telenet kiosks are located in the arrivals hall for prepaid tourist SIM cards.
A passport is required to register a Belgian SIM card.
